page: Microsoft Azure PB6s is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the PBS server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 Intel processor with 6 dedicated vCPUs and 112.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 18.67 GB per vCPU. This dense memory configuration is designed to support memory-intensive workloads without the overhead of excess compute resources.

The server includes 754 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, allowing for high-throughput disk configurations. There are no integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses bundled with this instance. The hardware profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring large memory footprints and local storage space relative to a moderate CPU count, avoiding the expense of higher-core instances.

Due to its high memory density and Premium Storage capabilities, the PB6s is suited for relational database hosting, in-memory caching layers, enterprise application backends, and data-intensive processing tasks that do not require massive parallel CPU scaling.
description: Microsoft Azure PB6s is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the PBS family, powered by an x86_64 Intel processor and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is configured with 6 dedicated vCPUs and a substantial 112.0 GB of RAM, providing an exceptional 18.67 GB of memory per core. The instance is equipped with 754 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, making it highly capable for disk-intensive operations. It does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy operations that do not require high CPU core counts. The PB6s is well-suited for workloads such as in-memory databases, caching systems, and enterprise application hosting.
